diagrams.title: Public Key Encryption Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the complete process of encrypting a message using the recipient's public key and decrypting it with their private key, demonstrating the asymmetric encryption concept.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how asymmetric encryption works - sender encrypts with public key, only recipient can decrypt with matching private key

diagrams.title: Digital Signature Process
diagrams.description: Illustrates how a sender creates a digital signature by hashing the message and encrypting the hash with their private key, and how the recipient verifies it using the sender's public key.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates authentication and integrity verification using digital signatures and the concept of non-repudiation

diagrams.title: Key Pair Relationship
diagrams.description: Visualizes the mathematical relationship between a public key and private key pair, showing that they are mathematically linked but computationally infeasible to derive one from the other.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the concept of key pairs, why the private key must remain secret, and how the public key can be freely shared
